title: Moroccan Apple Crumble
description: A traditional German dessert with a twist of Moroccan spices, topped with a crunchy crumble.
instructions: Peel, core, and slice the apples.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced apples, Moroccan spice blend, and granulated sugar. Mix until the apples are evenly coated.
Transfer the apple mixture to a baking dish.
Roll out the flaky pastry and cut it into strips.
Place the pastry strips on top of the apple mixture, weaving them together to form a lattice-top crust.
In a small bowl, mix together the tangy crumble topping ingredients until crumbly.
Sprinkle the crumble topping evenly over the pastry lattice.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the apples are tender.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar. Serve warm, topped with additional citrus zest if desired.
